auth.signInDescription: Sign in to upload and manage your team's data

footer.disclaimer: Twinspire is a decision support platform. It is not intended to diagnose or treat medical conditions.

hero.valueProp: GPS and training load from the club. Whoop, Oura, and Garmin from the player. Two streams that have never been connected properly. Twinspire connects them and builds an individual baseline for each athlete.
hero.credibility: Developed at DTU. Presented at ECSS Lausanne 2026.

problem.label: The Gap
problem.headline: Two Data Streams. Neither Aware of the Other.
problem.p1: Your club captures GPS, gym load, and physio notes. Your players privately measure Whoop recovery, Oura sleep, and Garmin HRV at 6am on a Sunday. These streams have never been connected with the consent architecture and individual modeling to make them clinically useful. That gap is where the early warning signals live.
problem.block1Body: Training load. GPS output. Session data. Physio observations. Valuable — but incomplete. It only captures what happens inside club hours.
problem.block2Body: Resting heart rate at home. Recovery scores on rest days. Sleep quality before a match. HRV at 6am. This data exists on the player's wrist. Nobody is reading it in context.
problem.block3Body: One in five footballers re-injures the same muscle. That rate has not moved in a decade — not because the data is absent, but because the individual has never been properly modelled across both streams over time.
problem.originHeading: The Data Existed. Nothing Read It.
problem.originBody: Twinspire began with a rehabilitation process that followed every expected protocol. A second injury followed. The signals had been there — in the recovery scores, in the HRV trends, in the load data. They existed in separate systems that never spoke to each other.

digitalTwin.sectionLabel: What a Decision Support System Actually Is
digitalTwin.heading: A dashboard displays variables.
digitalTwin.headingAccent: It does not explain what matters, why it matters, or what to do next.
digitalTwin.attribution: Dr. Lorena Martin, USC Marshall — ECSS Lausanne 2026
digitalTwin.intro: At ECSS 2026, independent researchers proposed a checklist for what a genuine individual athlete model requires. Below is where Twinspire stands against it — honestly.

individualized.label: Individualized
individualized.body: Every metric measured against that specific player's own evolving baseline — not a squad average, not a population norm.

dynamicallyUpdated.label: Dynamically Updated
dynamicallyUpdated.body: The model updates continuously with every new data point from both streams. The longer it runs for a player, the more precisely it understands them.

connectedToDecisions.label: Connected to Decisions
connectedToDecisions.body: Output is framed as a decision — is this player ready today — not as a data export or a chart to interpret.

explicitAboutUncertainty.label: Explicit About Uncertainty
explicitAboutUncertainty.body: Key drivers surfaced explicitly. Staff see which signals are driving any deviation — not just that a deviation exists.

validatedAgainstOutcomes.label: Validated Against Outcomes
validatedAgainstOutcomes.body: The specific gap the validation cohort is designed to close. We are honest about the fact that it is not yet fully met.

digitalTwin.closingNote: We use this checklist internally. We think you should hold us to it.

science.label: How It Works
science.heading: Club data. Personal data.
science.intro: Club data arrives via API — no platform switch required. Player data arrives via the Twinspire app, consented once. Both streams feed the individual baseline model. The output goes back into your existing setup.

modeling.title: Works With Your Current Setup
modeling.body: API integration with your existing platform — Catapult, Statsports, Bricks, or any provider with an open API. No migration. Outputs appear in your current interface.
modeling.bullet3: Outputs feed back into your current UI

signals.title: Connects the Private Data Stream
signals.body: Whoop, Oura, Garmin, Polar — the wearables your players use off-site. Twinspire is the only platform with the consent architecture to legitimately integrate this data into club analytics.
signals.bullet2: Player consents once — data stays theirs

validation.title: Individual Baseline Modeling
validation.body: What normal looks like for this specific player — not for footballers in general. Built from both streams, continuously updated, surfacing deviation early.

questions.q1: Is this player's current state within their personal normal range — combining club and personal data?
questions.q2: Which signals are driving any deviation, and from which stream?
questions.q3: Is this player ready to train at full intensity — by their individual model, not a population benchmark?

trust.body: GDPR-compliant from the ground up. EU servers. Player data is never sold. The passport belongs to the athlete — it moves when they transfer. The squad data belongs to the club.
trust.bullet1: GDPR compliance and Data Processing Agreements
trust.disclaimer: Twinspire is a decision support platform. It is not intended to diagnose or treat medical conditions.

product.p1: When your doctor runs blood tests, they track your values over time — because your normal might be different from the reference range. Twinspire does exactly that for every metric in your squad, across both the data the club controls and the data the player privately generates.

card1.title: Not a squad benchmark
card1.body: A shift that looks unremarkable across a squad can be a significant warning for a particular player. Each individual's reference behavior is built separately.

card2.title: Trained staff leave. The model does not.
card2.body: A good physio intuitively knows their long-term players. When staff turn over, that knowledge leaves. Twinspire makes it objective and permanent.

card3.title: The Athletic Passport
card3.body: Every session, load, injury, and rehab milestone — portable, owned by the athlete. When they transfer, their full history transfers. New club inherits objective data from day one.

product.headline2: No Extra App for the Player
product.p2: If your platform already has a player-facing app, Twinspire operates in the background. The player's wearables sync automatically. The output appears in your existing interface. Twinspire is invisible to the player's daily workflow.

passport.heading: Every time a player transfers,
passport.headingAccent: their physiological history resets to zero.
passport.intro: A new club inherits a name, a transfer fee, and a medical record. What they do not inherit is 18 months of individual baseline data. They start from scratch. Every time.
passport.howItWorks: The Athletic Passport changes that. Every session, load, injury, and rehabilitation milestone is structured into a portable, consent-based record that belongs to the athlete. When they sign for a new club, their baseline arrives with them.
passport.forClubs: For clubs: objective physiological data on every new signing from day one. For players: a career-long performance history that is portable and owned by them.
passport.differentiator: No platform presented at ECSS 2026 demonstrated cross-club physiological continuity built on a proper consent and GDPR architecture.

passportItems.item1: Individual baseline, continuously updated across every club
passportItems.item2: Full load, injury, and rehabilitation history
passportItems.item3: Portable consent — the athlete controls who sees what
passportItems.item4: New club inherits objective data from day one of signing

footballers.title: Footballers
footballers.description: Connect your Whoop, Oura, or Garmin once. Your individual baseline is yours — not the club's. When you move, it moves with you.

coaches.title: Coaches & Performance Staff
coaches.description: Squad-wide readiness built on individual baselines, not averages. Including the signal your current platform has no access to.

physio.title: Physiotherapists
physio.description: Deviation alerts across both club and personal data streams — before they become conversations, and well before they become injuries.

cta.title: We Are Selecting Founding Partner Clubs
cta.subtitle: Twinspire is at validation stage — deploying with a small number of clubs across the Nordics to close the outcomes validation gap. Founding partner clubs shape the product, contribute to the dataset, and secure preferential terms ahead of commercial launch.
cta.note: No commitment. A short call to understand your setup and whether this is the right fit.

partners.heading: Platform and Sensor Partners.
partners.body: Built to integrate, not compete. If your customers are asking questions squad-level data cannot answer at the individual level — let us talk.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: The questions clubs, athletes, and partners ask us most often.

whatIsTwinspire.question: What is Twinspire?
whatIsTwinspire.answer: Twinspire connects the data your club platform captures with the personal wearable data your players generate off-site — and builds a continuously updated individual baseline model for each athlete. The output feeds back into your existing setup as readiness scores, deviation alerts, and trend data.

whatStageIsThisAt.question: What stage is Twinspire at?
whatStageIsThisAt.answer: Validation stage. The platform is deployed with a founding partner cohort of clubs across the Nordics. The purpose of this cohort is to close the outcomes validation gap — the one criterion on the external checklist we are honest about not yet fully meeting. We are not claiming live commercial traction we do not have.

doWeNeedToSwitchPlatforms.question: Do we need to switch platforms?
doWeNeedToSwitchPlatforms.answer: No. Twinspire integrates with your current setup via API. Whether you use Catapult, Statsports, Bricks, or a custom platform — Twinspire sits underneath it, returning outputs into your existing interface.

whatAboutThePlayerApp.question: We already give our players an app. We cannot ask them to use two.
whatAboutThePlayerApp.answer: If you have an existing player-facing app, Twinspire operates entirely in the background. The player's wearables sync through a one-time consent setup. Readiness and deviation data appears in your staff dashboard — not in a separate player interface. Twinspire is invisible to the player's daily workflow.

whatWearablesDoYouSupport.question: Which wearables does Twinspire integrate with?
whatWearablesDoYouSupport.answer: Whoop, Oura, Garmin, and Polar are currently supported. These are the devices your players most commonly use privately — the ones capturing recovery, sleep, and HRV data off-site that your club platform has no access to.

whatIsAthleticPassport.question: What is the Athletic Passport?
whatIsAthleticPassport.answer: Every time a player transfers, their physiological history resets to zero. The Athletic Passport changes that — a portable, consent-based record of everything a player's body has generated. It belongs to the athlete. When they sign for a new club, their baseline arrives with them.

howDoesExplainabilityWork.question: Is the model a black box?
howDoesExplainabilityWork.answer: No. When a deviation is flagged, staff see which specific signals are driving it — whether from club-side load data, personal HRV, recovery scores, or a combination. The output is a decision with visible reasoning, not a score to interpret.

whoOwnsData.question: Who owns the player data?
whoOwnsData.answer: The player owns their Athletic Passport. The club owns its squad analytics. Twinspire hosts both under a formal Data Processing Agreement. Player data is never sold, never shared, and fully portable when a player transfers.

isTwinspireGdprCompliant.question: Is Twinspire GDPR compliant?
isTwinspireGdprCompliant.answer: Yes, and built to a higher standard than most sports platforms — medical device regulation principles, full GDPR compliance, EU-based hosting, and formal DPAs for all club integrations.

canWePartnerWithTwinspire.question: We are a platform provider. Can we partner?
canWePartnerWithTwinspire.answer: Yes. If you run a club performance platform and your customers are asking questions squad-level data cannot answer individually — or you want to add private wearable integration with proper consent management — reach out at info@twinspire.ai.

whereIsTwinspireBased.question: Where is Twinspire based?
whereIsTwinspireBased.answer: Founded at the Technical University of Denmark (DTU) in Copenhagen. Started by Pouya Tobias Strand Nikoui — a former professional footballer who suffered a reinjury after full clinical clearance, and returned to DTU to build what the sport was missing. Backed by DTU Skylab and DIF Innovation Lab.

howToGetStarted.question: How do we get started?
howToGetStarted.answer: Apply to join the founding partner cohort via the form on this page, or contact us directly at info@twinspire.ai.

dataEthics.heading: The Compliance Architecture
dataEthics.subtitle: GDPR, medical device standards, and data ownership — designed in from the start.
dataEthics.description: As player unions and club legal teams scrutinise health data handling, the compliance architecture your platform is built on matters.
dataEthics.card1: The Athletic Passport belongs to the athlete. The squad analytics belong to the club. Twinspire hosts both under formal Data Processing Agreements. Neither is ever sold or shared.
dataEthics.card2: GDPR-aligned from the ground up. EU-based hosting. Medical device regulation principles. Formal DPAs for all club integrations. Granular consent controls.
dataEthics.card3: The model is not a black box. Every deviation flag surfaces its key drivers — which signals, from which stream, in which direction. Staff see the reasoning, not just the output.
dataEthics.card4: Twinspire surfaces signals for qualified staff to act on. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, or replace clinical judgment.
dataEthics.disclaimer: Twinspire is a decision support platform. It is not intended to diagnose or treat medical conditions.

team.heading: Built at DTU.
team.headingAccent: By People Who Have Lived the Problem.
team.subtitle: Founded at the Technical University of Denmark. Backed by DTU Skylab and DIF Innovation Lab.

descriptions.pouya: Pouya played professional football until a reinjury ended his season after a full clinical clearance. The signals had been there — across separate systems that never spoke to each other. He returned to DTU, turned that experience into a research question, and built what the sport was missing. MSc in Electrical Engineering specialising in AI and control systems. He leads research translation, strategic direction, and external partnerships.
descriptions.john: Professor at DTU leading research into digital twin methodology for physiological systems — the scientific foundation of Twinspire. His neuromuscular research group is where the project was born. Beyond research, John has built and sold technology companies with exits to Facebook, Oculus, and GN Audio.
descriptions.daryan: Seven years building software at the intersection of architecture, data engineering, and people leadership. At Twinspire he leads the technical implementation connecting club data sources, wearable integrations, and the platform infrastructure that staff and athletes use daily.
descriptions.roxane: Built and taken AI ventures to market across three continents. Previously co-founded CUJU, an AI sports scouting company developed with ROGON Sports Management, from zero to exit. Worked with the UN and World Bank on innovation programme development. At Twinspire she leads fundraising, partnerships, and commercial strategy.

joinUs.pageTitleAccent: Help Build the Intelligence Layer Elite Football Is Missing.
joinUs.pageSubtitle: A small team solving a hard technical problem where the stakes are real.
joinUs.heroSubtitle: Built by people who understand the problem from the inside.
